questions.question: What type of relationship drives traceability in Codebeamer?
questions.rationale: Reference relationships (upstream/downstream) are the primary relationship type that drives traceability in Codebeamer. Parent-child relationships are organizational only within a tracker. Document numbers are view-dependent labels, and comment links are informal and not maintained by Codebeamer.

questions.question: What happens to downstream items when an upstream reference is changed in Codebeamer?
options: Downstream items are automatically updated to match
Downstream items are deleted and must be recreated
Downstream items receive a suspected badge
Downstream items are moved to a different tracker
questions.rationale: When an upstream item changes, downstream items receive a suspected badge indicating they need to be reviewed. The downstream items are not automatically updated or deleted — the badge alerts users that the change may affect the downstream item's validity.

questions.question: In the exercise project, what prefix identifies a Customer Requirement Specification?
questions.rationale: In the exercise project, Customer Requirement Specifications use the prefix CRS. System Requirement Specifications use SRS. The prefix is determined by the tracker's Key field in its configuration and is used to quickly identify which tracker an item belongs to.

questions.question: Why should you NOT use document numbers when discussing tracker items with coworkers?
options: Document numbers are not visible to other users
Document numbers change depending on the tracker view being used
Document numbers are only available in the admin panel
Document numbers are assigned randomly and have no meaning
questions.rationale: Document numbers vary depending on the tracker view. In a basic document view of System Requirements, an item might be number 2.2, but in a traceability view of Customer Requirements, the same item could be 1.1. Use the tracker item ID or summary name instead for unambiguous communication.
